sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.72 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: Let$G_1$and$G_2$be Lie groups with the following property: each$G_i$contains a nilpotent simply connected normal Lie subgroup$B_i$such that$G_i/B_i SL_2(K)$, where$K={ R}$or${ C}$. Assume that$G_1$and$G_2$are contained as closed subgroups in a topological group$G$, that$G_1 G_2 B_1B_2$, and that no non-identity Lie subgroup of$B_1 B_2$is normal in$G$. reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
